report:
## Your Health Assessment

Thank you for sharing your health picture with us. Your energy score of 3/10 alongside your digestive concerns and eating patterns reveal some meaningful connections that we often see working together.

### Irregular Fueling, Inconsistent Energy

Your eating pattern—skipping or minimizing breakfast and lunch, then having a large late dinner with evening snacking—creates significant gaps in fuel availability throughout the day. When the body doesn't receive consistent energy from food, it relies on stress hormones like cortisol and adrenaline to maintain blood sugar. This adaptive mechanism works in the short term but depletes your reserves over time, which aligns directly with your goal of achieving more consistent energy throughout the day.

### Digestive Stress Signals

The combination of straining, incomplete emptying, and diarrhea tells us your gut is working under suboptimal conditions. These seemingly contradictory symptoms—constipation signs alongside loose stools—often point to disrupted gut motility and potential microbial imbalances. When digestion is compromised, nutrient absorption suffers, which can further drain your available energy and affect sleep quality, both of which you've identified as priorities.

### The Evening Compensation Cycle

Eating most of your calories late in the day, particularly after periods of under-eating, can create a pattern where your body is doing heavy digestive work when it should be winding down for restorative sleep. This timing mismatch may be contributing to both your low daytime energy and the sleep quality improvements you're seeking. Your system is essentially running on stress during the day and digestion at night—the opposite of what supports optimal function.

---

These patterns often interconnect in ways that a comprehensive consultation can fully uncover. We'd love to explore your unique situation in more depth.

[Book a consultation](https://prism.miami/booking) to start your journey toward better health.
